H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble) is a strong-confidence protocol (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, pct_pos=66%) with the highest n in HEALING series. Qualitative (n=29) shows settle_time: 10 min (11), 5 min (7). Activity contexts: driving (5), just listening (5). Music: liked (10), loved (9). Rhythm: balanced (10), calming (8). AutoGluon predicted 6.86 — actual 7.15 exceeds by 0.29, indicating positive efficacy signal. YouTube views=30 (highest across all protocols) but retention data pending.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble is the standout performer in the overstimulation category — strong outcomes with the most listener data, and many people discover it while driving.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall) shows strong confidence (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, pct_pos=65%). Qualitative (n=21): settle_time 10 min (10), immediate (3). Activity: relaxing (3), just listening (3). Music: liked (10), neutral (6). Rhythm: calming (7), balanced (7). AutoGluon predicted 6.89. YouTube views=5. No anomalies detected.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall provides solid overstimulation relief with most people settling within 10 minutes; the music is well-liked overall.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier) has meaningful confidence (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=23): settle_time: 5 min (7), 10 min (5). Activity: trying to sleep (8), working (4). Music: liked (11), neutral (5). Rhythm: balanced (10), calming (10). AutoGluon predicted 6.82. YouTube views=8. Clusters with X-ME-L2 in embedding analysis (0.882 similarity), suggesting potential formula overlap.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier works well especially for sleep, with a balanced mix of calming and comfortable rhythm opinions — appears to share some design elements with Sunrise.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow) demonstrates strong confidence (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, pct_pos=63%). Qualitative data from 23 respondents shows balanced rhythm (13/23) and calming settle times (9 settled within 10 min). AutoGluon predicted score 7.84 vs observed 7.22 — delta suggests potential upward reach with expanded volume exposure. YouTube views=7 with pending retention data. Panel divided: two analyzers flagged adviser-driven sample skew, others saw routing as appropriate.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow is one of the most effective anxiety protocols — most listeners feel calm within about 10 minutes, with the rhythm described as comfortable and balanced.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (Cottage) shows meaningful confidence (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, pct_pos=67%). Listener split: 12 self, 6 client/patient, 2 supported. Settle times averaging 5-10 min. Music_opinion skewed neutral (5/20) vs liked (9/20). AutoGluon predicted 7.02. YouTube views=6 with pending retention. No major anomalies detected across panel readings.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage works well for anxiety but settles slightly slower than Meadow; some listeners found the music just okay, suggesting personal preference plays a role.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (Valley) achieves meaningful confidence (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, pct_pos=61%). Qualitative shows 10-min settle (10/20) as dominant pattern with balanced rhythm (10/20). Music_opinion split between liked (8) and neutral (5). YouTube views=4. Panel notes higher 'no effect' responses (2) than L1/L2, suggesting possible entrainment curve sensitivity.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley shows good anxiety relief but has more listeners reporting 'no effect' than its siblings, so it may work better for some people than others.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (Anchor) maintains meaningful confidence despite acute distress context (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.7, pct_pos=53%). This mean is structurally expected for crisis protocols — listeners arrive in extreme states. Qualitative shows significant improvement (8/24) despite lower numeric score. Settle_times: 5 min (8), 10 min (5). YouTube views=6. Panel notes routing appears appropriate given crisis population baseline.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor is designed for crisis moments and performs as expected — people in acute distress often start with lower scores, but many still report meaningful improvement.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (Harbor) shows meaningful confidence (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.7, pct_pos=55%). Qualitative data (n=16) shows balanced rhythm (6/16) and calming (8/16) with mixed music_opinion: neutral (6) vs loved (5). Settle_time: 10 min (5), 5 min (3). YouTube views=4. No anomalies flagged.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor provides calm during crisis with most people settling around the 10-minute mark; the rhythm is generally described as very settling.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (Horizon) has meaningful confidence (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, pct_pos=52%) with lowest positive pct in crisis series. Qualitative shows immediate settle (7/19) most common — fastest acting in series. Music_opinion split: liked (7), not liked (4). YouTube views=5. Panel notes high variance (std=1.91) suggests possible population split deserving monitoring.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon works fastest in the crisis series (some feel better within the first minute) though results vary more widely than other crisis protocols.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern) achieves strong confidence (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, pct_pos=65%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 5 min (5), immediate (3). Activity: therapy (3), studying (3). Music: liked (10), loved (3). Rhythm: balanced (7), calming (4). AutoGluon predicted 7.54 (highest in sleep zone). YouTube views=4 — limited reach signal. Panel notes: highest cross-run drift (sim=0.634) among all protocols, indicating evolving interpretation patterns requiring monitoring.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern shows excellent sleep results with fast settlement times, though it has the most analytical variation over time — a protocol to monitor closely.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock) has meaningful confidence (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, pct_pos=67%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 10 min (7), 20 min (3). Activity: studying (4), driving (3). Music: neutral (6), liked (5). Rhythm: balanced (8), too busy (3). Note: listener mix shows 6 client/patient, 5 supported, 4 self — high proportion of non-self listeners may influence scoring interpretation. AutoGluon predicted 7.61. YouTube views=5.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock works but has a higher proportion of non-self listeners (clients, people being supported), which may affect how scoring patterns develop.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight) shows meaningful confidence (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=22): settle_time 5 min (8), 10 min (5). Activity: relaxing (5), caregiving (4). Music: liked (8), neutral (6). Rhythm: balanced (9), busy (7). AutoGluon predicted 7.54. YouTube views=3. Embedding drift (sim=0.731) flagged but within acceptable range.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight provides good sleep support with most people settling within 5-10 minutes; music opinions lean more neutral than its siblings.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass) has meaningful confidence (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, pct_pos=56%). Qualitative (n=23): settle_time 5 min (7), immediate (6). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: liked (15), neutral (4). Rhythm: slightly busy (9), calming (6). AutoGluon predicted 6.88. YouTube views=5. Panel notes 'slightly busy' rhythm opinions may indicate entrainment curve mismatch for certain listener subgroups.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass is the entry-level focus protocol with moderate outcomes; some listeners find the rhythm slightly too active for deep concentration.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint) shows meaningful confidence (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, pct_pos=66%). Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 10 min (9), 5 min (4). Activity: caregiving (5), therapy (4). Music: neutral (6), loved (5). Rhythm: balanced (8), slightly busy (5). AutoGluon predicted 7.31. YouTube views=4. Notable improvement from L1 (6.46 → 7.19).
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint shows meaningful improvement over Level 1 for focus, with most people settling around 10 minutes and balanced rhythm opinions.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit) achieves meaningful confidence with highest THRIVING mean (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, pct_pos=71%). Qualitative (n=29): settle_time 10 min (15), 5 min (6). Activity: relaxing (4), chores (4). Music: liked (12), loved (8). Rhythm: balanced (15), slightly busy (7). 'Significant improvement' (11), 'noticeable' (7). AutoGluon predicted 7.73. YouTube views=15 (highest in THRIVING). This protocol is a strong candidate for THRIVING-to-TRANSFORMING bridging given its high efficacy and confidence level despite higher variance.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit is the highest-performing focus protocol across all series, with the most 'significant improvement' reports and strong listener satisfaction.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember) has meaningful confidence (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, pct_pos=53%). Qualitative (n=21): settle_time immediate (6), 10 min (6). Activity: relaxing (4), therapy (3). Music: liked (9), loved (6). Rhythm: balanced (9), calming (6). 'Slight improvement' reported most frequently (8/21). AutoGluon predicted 6.86. YouTube views=2. Embedding clusters with T-CF-L3 (0.895 similarity).
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember works for creative flow but shows modest improvement levels with some listeners reporting only slight gains; the rhythm feels balanced.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current) shows meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, pct_pos=58%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 10 min (5), 5 min (4). Activity: driving (3), sleep (2). Music: loved (6), liked (6). Rhythm: balanced (6), calming (5). Highest pct_pos in L1-L3 range. AutoGluon predicted 6.91. YouTube views=2. No anomalies noted.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current is the mid-tier creative flow protocol with notably better outcomes than Level 1, and more people love the music here.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora) demonstrates developing but promising confidence (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, pct_pos=67%) — highest THRIVING mean. Qualitative (n=25 due to contextual sample): settle_time 10 min (7), 5 min (7). Activity: sleep (5), working (4). Music: liked (9), neutral (7). Rhythm: balanced (14), calming (6). 'Noticeable improvement' (7), 'significant' (6). AutoGluon predicted 7.42 for focus zone. This protocol shows strongest cross-series bridging potential given high mean, low variance, and consistent qualitative signals. YouTube views=7.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora shows the strongest creative flow results in the entire series despite having fewer responses — a promising protocol worth more exposure.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone) shows meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=23): settle_time 20 min (9), 5 min (6). Activity: therapy (8), sleep (4). Music: neutral (6), liked (6). Rhythm: balanced (12), calming (4). Longest dominant settle_time (20 min) in THRIVING. AutoGluon predicted 7.23. YouTube views=3. Embedding drift flagged (0.742).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone works well but takes longer to settle (20 minutes is typical); designed for ongoing maintenance rather than quick results.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis) has meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, pct_pos=59%) with widest THRIVING variance. Qualitative (n=16): settle_time 5 min (6), 10 min (4). Activity: working (4), sleep (3). Music: liked (9), neutral (4). Rhythm: balanced (7), slightly busy (7). AutoGluon predicted 6.95. YouTube views=4. High std (2.03) indicates responder sensitivity — possible population subgroup effect.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis shows the widest variance in maintenance outcomes — works great for some but less consistently for others, indicating possible subgroup effects.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit) has developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, pct_pos=71%). Qualitative (n=19): settle_time 5 min (8), did NOT settle (4). Activity: studying (4), caregiving (3). Music: liked (7), neutral (4). Rhythm: balanced (7), calming (5). 'Did NOT settle' responses (4/19) elevated for Maintenance series. AutoGluon predicted 7.34. YouTube views=3. High embedding drift (0.700) signals analytical instability.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit has some listeners who don't settle at all, which is unusual for maintenance; may need adjustment for the non-responding subgroup.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) has meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, pct_pos=54%). Qualitative (n=22): settle_time 20 min (6), 10 min (5). Activity: caregiving (5), just listening (3). Music: loved (10), neutral (6). Rhythm: balanced (11), calming (5). AutoGluon predicted top cross-bucket score (8.23) for low-affect states. YouTube views=8. Cross-bucket routing to X-ME-L1 indicates strong efficacy signal.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze is a mood and energy protocol with strong cross-series signals — its AutoGluon score suggests it may help low-affect states better than designed.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) shows meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 20 min (4), immediate (4). Activity: sleep (2), therapy (2). Music: liked (7), loved (6). Rhythm: balanced (7), slightly busy (3). AutoGluon predicts cross-bucket routing to mood_energy zone. YouTube views=8. Embedding clusters with H-OS-L3 (0.882 similarity).
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise provides solid mood support with strong music appreciation; seems to share design elements with Glacier in the overstimulation series.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) has developing confidence (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, pct_pos=56%). Qualitative (n=25): settle_time 5 min (8), 10 min (6). Activity: relaxing (5), chores (5). Music: liked (11), loved (7). Rhythm: calming (9), balanced (9). 'No effect' responses (5/25) elevated. AutoGluon predicted 7.88. YouTube views=4. Highest 'no effect' rate in TRANSFORMING suggests possible entrainment curve mismatch.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft shows the highest 'no effect' rate in TRANSFORMING — may need rhythm adjustment for better entrainment consistency.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) has meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, pct_pos=45%) — lowest positive pct in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 5 min (9), immediate (4). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: liked (8), neutral (5). Rhythm: balanced (7), calming (6). Lower pct_pos reflects ASCENT protocol challenges. AutoGluon predicted 7.88 for motivation zone. YouTube views=5.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath has the lowest positive response rate in TRANSFORMING; motivation protocols are harder to deliver consistently.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail) shows developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, pct_pos=43%). Qualitative (n=21): settle_time 20 min (5), 10 min (5). Activity: studying (5), sleep (3). Music: loved (9), neutral (5). Rhythm: balanced (9), slightly busy (6). This protocol shows unusual positive music_opinion despite moderate numeric scores. AutoGluon predicted 7.65. YouTube views=7.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail shows an interesting split where people love the music but report more moderate improvement; a possible design mismatch.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) has developing confidence (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, pct_pos=62%) — highest TRANSFORMING mean despite low n. Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 5 min (5), immediate (4). Activity: sleep (6), relaxing (4). Music: liked (6), loved (4). Rhythm: balanced (8), slightly busy (4). AutoGluon predicted 7.88 — actual 7.15 represents 0.73 delta. Bimodal distribution suggests subgroup effect; embedding drift (0.675) confirms analytical instability. YouTube views=7.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback is the highest TRANSFORMING mean but with high variance — works very well for some but less so for others, indicating population split.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern) has meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, pct_pos=54%). Qualitative (n=25): settle_time 10 min (8), 5 min (8). Activity: just listening (5), caregiving (5). Music: loved (9), liked (9). Rhythm: balanced (9), calming (8). Two respondents (2/25) reported 'made things worse.' AutoGluon predicted 7.12. YouTube views=8. Within expected TRANSFORMING variance bounds.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern is a solid mind clarity protocol with two listeners noting things felt worse — unusual but worth monitoring given small sample.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) shows developing confidence (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, pct_pos=56%). Qualitative (n=20): settle_time 10 min (6), immediate (6). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: loved (6), liked (6). Rhythm: calming (12), balanced (4). 'Significant improvement' (7) is highest signal. AutoGluon predicted 7.54. YouTube views=12. No anomalies.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight shows strong 'significant improvement' signals despite moderate average scores, with most listeners finding it very calming.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) is a developing-confidence outlier (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, pct_pos=50%) — the lowest mean across all protocols. Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 5 min (6), 10 min (4). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: liked (6), neutral (5). Rhythm: calming (6), too busy (4). Two 'no effect' responses. AutoGluon predicted 7.54 for mind_clarity zone — delta of -0.84 is the largest negative deviation in the dataset. Panel convergence: all five analyzers flagged this as requiring redesign or routing adjustment. Embedding similarity (0.677 drift) confirms analytical isolation.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos is the clear underperformer — the lowest-scoring protocol overall. It may need redesign or better routing to the right listener subgroup.

sphere_commentary.overview:
This run (n=1000) shows HEALING achieving stable outcomes across 12 protocols, THRIVING delivering higher peaks but with wider variance, and TRANSFORMING showing expected lower means with highest spread — consistent with ascending-arc design. The protocol-level analysis reveals 3 standout performers (T-FC-L3 at 7.63, H-AX-L1 at 7.22, H-OS-L1 at 7.15) and several developing signals requiring monitoring.
• Mean scores across series: HEALING 6.98, THRIVING 6.96, TRANSFORMING 6.59
• Highest variance observed in TRANSFORMING protocols (std 1.87)
• YouTube engagement data remains sparse, limiting retention signal analysis
• AutoGluon identifies avg_view_percentage as strongest predictor of outcomes
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING protocols demonstrate consistent efficacy with the lowest variance across all series — the descent architecture appears well-calibrated for calming applications. THRIVING shows higher peaks but also wider spread, particularly in Maintenance & Balance protocols (T-MB-L2 std=2.03), suggesting responder sensitivity. TRANSFORMING carries the expected highest variance due to its ascending-arc design targeting low-affect states, but X-MC-L3's underperformance (5.7 mean) exceeds expected variance bounds.
• HEALING: n=574, mean=6.98, std=1.77 — stable regulatory intent delivery
• THRIVING: n=245, mean=6.96, std=1.72 — highest single peak (T-FC-L3 at 7.63)
• TRANSFORMING: n=181, mean=6.59, std=1.87 — design-intent variance + X-MC-L3 anomaly
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
X-MC-L3 represents a clear outlier requiring investigation — its 5.7 mean (n=10) falls 1.09 points below series average and shows bimodal distribution. Panel divided on X-MD-L3: two analyzers flagged bimodal split (7.15 vs 4.6 subsets), while others noted it as within expected variance. H-CR-L1 shows 53% positive outcomes, which is structurally expected for acute distress protocols but below HEALING series average.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) — clear underperformer, lowest across all protocols
• X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38) — bimodal distribution suggests population split
• H-CR-L1 (n=43, mean=6.35) — expected low for crisis, but below HEALING average
• X-ME-L3 shows unusual bimodal music_opinion split
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Four converging development signals emerge from the panel. X-MC-L3 requires urgent redesign — all five analyzers independently flagged its underperformance (mean 5.7 vs 6.59 series average). T-FC-L3 warrants investigation as a THRIVING-to-TRANSFORMING bridge candidate given its 7.63 mean with strong confidence. H-SL-L1 shows high embedding drift (sim=0.634) suggesting evolving response patterns that may benefit from hold-phase adjustment. The X-MD series bimodal split suggests need for stratified routing.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 — redesign required; investigate entrainment curve, groove style, or routing mismatch
• T-FC-L3 — investigate as cross-series bridging candidate given 7.63 strong performance
• H-SL-L1 — monitor embedding drift; consider extending hold phase duration
• X-MD-L1/L2 series — implement stratified routing based on listener baseline affect
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals notable cross-run drift in several protocols, with H-SL-L1 showing the highest drift (sim=0.634 between runs), followed by X-MD-L3 (0.675) and X-MC-L3 (0.677). Cross-protocol clusters reveal tighter semantic groupings than expected: H-OS-L3 clusters with X-ME-L2 (sim=0.882), suggesting possible formula overlap. X-MC-L3 and X-MD-L3 remain analytically isolated (mean_sim 0.640 and 0.691 respectively), consistent with their status as series outliers.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: H-SL-L1 (0.634), X-MD-L3 (0.675), X-MC-L3 (0.677) show notable drift requiring monitoring
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: H-OS-L3 ↔ X-ME-L2 (0.882), T-CF-L1 ↔ T-CF-L3 (0.895) show unexpected coupling
• Confidence note: Limited to 3 volume runs; drift signals require validation with additional chronological data

Activity: trying to sleep (8), working (4). Music: liked (11), neutral (5). Rhythm: balanced (10), calming (10). AutoGluon predicted 6.82. YouTube views=8. Clusters with X-ME-L2 in embedding analysis (0.882 similarity), suggesting potential formula overlap.
by_protocol.H-SL-L1: H-SL-L1 (Lantern) achieves strong confidence (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, pct_pos=65%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 5 min (5), immediate (3). Activity: therapy (3), studying (3). Music: liked (10), loved (3). Rhythm: balanced (7), calming (4). AutoGluon predicted 7.54 (highest in sleep zone). YouTube views=4 — limited reach signal. Panel notes: highest cross-run drift (sim=0.634) among all protocols, indicating evolving interpretation patterns requiring monitoring.
by_protocol.H-SL-L2: H-SL-L2 (Hammock) has meaningful confidence (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, pct_pos=67%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 10 min (7), 20 min (3). Activity: studying (4), driving (3). Music: neutral (6), liked (5). Rhythm: balanced (8), too busy (3). Note: listener mix shows 6 client/patient, 5 supported, 4 self — high proportion of non-self listeners may influence scoring interpretation. AutoGluon predicted 7.61. YouTube views=5.
by_protocol.H-SL-L3: H-SL-L3 (Midnight) shows meaningful confidence (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=22): settle_time 5 min (8), 10 min (5). Activity: relaxing (5), caregiving (4). Music: liked (8), neutral (6). Rhythm: balanced (9), busy (7). AutoGluon predicted 7.54. YouTube views=3. Embedding drift (sim=0.731) flagged but within acceptable range.
by_protocol.T-CF-L1: T-CF-L1 (Ember) has meaningful confidence (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, pct_pos=53%). Qualitative (n=21): settle_time immediate (6), 10 min (6). Activity: relaxing (4), therapy (3). Music: liked (9), loved (6). Rhythm: balanced (9), calming (6). 'Slight improvement' reported most frequently (8/21). AutoGluon predicted 6.86. YouTube views=2. Embedding clusters with T-CF-L3 (0.895 similarity).
by_protocol.T-CF-L2: T-CF-L2 (Current) shows meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, pct_pos=58%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 10 min (5), 5 min (4). Activity: driving (3), sleep (2). Music: loved (6), liked (6). Rhythm: balanced (6), calming (5). Highest pct_pos in L1-L3 range. AutoGluon predicted 6.91. YouTube views=2. No anomalies noted.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: T-CF-L3 (Aurora) demonstrates developing but promising confidence (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, pct_pos=67%) — highest THRIVING mean. Qualitative (n=25 due to contextual sample): settle_time 10 min (7), 5 min (7). Activity: sleep (5), working (4). Music: liked (9), neutral (7). Rhythm: balanced (14), calming (6). 'Noticeable improvement' (7), 'significant' (6). AutoGluon predicted 7.42 for focus zone. This protocol shows strongest cross-series bridging potential given high mean, low variance, and consistent qualitative signals. YouTube views=7.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: T-FC-L1 (Compass) has meaningful confidence (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, pct_pos=56%). Qualitative (n=23): settle_time 5 min (7), immediate (6). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: liked (15), neutral (4). Rhythm: slightly busy (9), calming (6). AutoGluon predicted 6.88. YouTube views=5. Panel notes 'slightly busy' rhythm opinions may indicate entrainment curve mismatch for certain listener subgroups.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint) shows meaningful confidence (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, pct_pos=66%). Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 10 min (9), 5 min (4). Activity: caregiving (5), therapy (4). Music: neutral (6), loved (5). Rhythm: balanced (8), slightly busy (5). AutoGluon predicted 7.31. YouTube views=4. Notable improvement from L1 (6.46 → 7.19).
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: T-FC-L3 (Summit) achieves meaningful confidence with highest THRIVING mean (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, pct_pos=71%). Qualitative (n=29): settle_time 10 min (15), 5 min (6). Activity: relaxing (4), chores (4). Music: liked (12), loved (8). Rhythm: balanced (15), slightly busy (7). 'Significant improvement' (11), 'noticeable' (7). AutoGluon predicted 7.73. YouTube views=15 (highest in THRIVING). This protocol is a strong candidate for THRIVING-to-TRANSFORMING bridging given its high efficacy and confidence level despite higher variance.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: T-MB-L1 (Stone) shows meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=23): settle_time 20 min (9), 5 min (6). Activity: therapy (8), sleep (4). Music: neutral (6), liked (6). Rhythm: balanced (12), calming (4). Longest dominant settle_time (20 min) in THRIVING. AutoGluon predicted 7.23. YouTube views=3. Embedding drift flagged (0.742).
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: T-MB-L2 (Axis) has meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, pct_pos=59%) with widest THRIVING variance. Qualitative (n=16): settle_time 5 min (6), 10 min (4). Activity: working (4), sleep (3). Music: liked (9), neutral (4). Rhythm: balanced (7), slightly busy (7). AutoGluon predicted 6.95. YouTube views=4. High std (2.03) indicates responder sensitivity — possible population subgroup effect.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: T-MB-L3 (Orbit) has developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, pct_pos=71%). Qualitative (n=19): settle_time 5 min (8), did NOT settle (4). Activity: studying (4), caregiving (3). Music: liked (7), neutral (4). Rhythm: balanced (7), calming (5). 'Did NOT settle' responses (4/19) elevated for Maintenance series. AutoGluon predicted 7.34. YouTube views=3. High embedding drift (0.700) signals analytical instability.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: X-MC-L1 (Cavern) has meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, pct_pos=54%). Qualitative (n=25): settle_time 10 min (8), 5 min (8). Activity: just listening (5), caregiving (5). Music: loved (9), liked (9). Rhythm: balanced (9), calming (8). Two respondents (2/25) reported 'made things worse.' AutoGluon predicted 7.12. YouTube views=8. Within expected TRANSFORMING variance bounds.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) shows developing confidence (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, pct_pos=56%). Qualitative (n=20): settle_time 10 min (6), immediate (6). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: loved (6), liked (6). Rhythm: calming (12), balanced (4). 'Significant improvement' (7) is highest signal. AutoGluon predicted 7.54. YouTube views=12. No anomalies.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) is a developing-confidence outlier (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, pct_pos=50%) — the lowest mean across all protocols. Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 5 min (6), 10 min (4). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: liked (6), neutral (5). Rhythm: calming (6), too busy (4). Two 'no effect' responses. AutoGluon predicted 7.54 for mind_clarity zone — delta of -0.84 is the largest negative deviation in the dataset. Panel convergence: all five analyzers flagged this as requiring redesign or routing adjustment. Embedding similarity (0.677 drift) confirms analytical isolation.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) has meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, pct_pos=45%) — lowest positive pct in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 5 min (9), immediate (4). Activity: studying (4), relaxing (3). Music: liked (8), neutral (5). Rhythm: balanced (7), calming (6). Lower pct_pos reflects ASCENT protocol challenges. AutoGluon predicted 7.88 for motivation zone. YouTube views=5.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 (Trail) shows developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, pct_pos=43%). Qualitative (n=21): settle_time 20 min (5), 10 min (5). Activity: studying (5), sleep (3). Music: loved (9), neutral (5). Rhythm: balanced (9), slightly busy (6). This protocol shows unusual positive music_opinion despite moderate numeric scores. AutoGluon predicted 7.65. YouTube views=7.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) has developing confidence (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, pct_pos=62%) — highest TRANSFORMING mean despite low n. Qualitative (n=17): settle_time 5 min (5), immediate (4). Activity: sleep (6), relaxing (4). Music: liked (6), loved (4). Rhythm: balanced (8), slightly busy (4). AutoGluon predicted 7.88 — actual 7.15 represents 0.73 delta. Bimodal distribution suggests subgroup effect; embedding drift (0.675) confirms analytical instability. YouTube views=7.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) has meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, pct_pos=54%). Qualitative (n=22): settle_time 20 min (6), 10 min (5). Activity: caregiving (5), just listening (3). Music: loved (10), neutral (6). Rhythm: balanced (11), calming (5). AutoGluon predicted top cross-bucket score (8.23) for low-affect states. YouTube views=8. Cross-bucket routing to X-ME-L1 indicates strong efficacy signal.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) shows meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, pct_pos=64%). Qualitative (n=15): settle_time 20 min (4), immediate (4). Activity: sleep (2), therapy (2). Music: liked (7), loved (6). Rhythm: balanced (7), slightly busy (3). AutoGluon predicts cross-bucket routing to mood_energy zone. YouTube views=8. Embedding clusters with H-OS-L3 (0.882 similarity).
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) has developing confidence (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, pct_pos=56%). Qualitative (n=25): settle_time 5 min (8), 10 min (6). Activity: relaxing (5), chores (5). Music: liked (11), loved (7). Rhythm: calming (9), balanced (9). 'No effect' responses (5/25) elevated. AutoGluon predicted 7.88. YouTube views=4. Highest 'no effect' rate in TRANSFORMING suggests possible entrainment curve mismatch.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow is one of the most effective anxiety protocols — most listeners feel calm within about 10 minutes, with the rhythm described as comfortable and balanced.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage works well for anxiety but settles slightly slower than Meadow; some listeners found the music just okay, suggesting personal preference plays a role.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley shows good anxiety relief but has more listeners reporting 'no effect' than its siblings, so it may work better for some people than others.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor is designed for crisis moments and performs as expected — people in acute distress often start with lower scores, but many still report meaningful improvement.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor provides calm during crisis with most people settling around the 10-minute mark; the rhythm is generally described as very settling.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon works fastest in the crisis series (some feel better within the first minute) though results vary more widely than other crisis protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble is the standout performer in the overstimulation category — strong outcomes with the most listener data, and many people discover it while driving.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall provides solid overstimulation relief with most people settling within 10 minutes; the music is well-liked overall.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier works well especially for sleep, with a balanced mix of calming and comfortable rhythm opinions — appears to share some design elements with Sunrise.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern shows excellent sleep results with fast settlement times, though it has the most analytical variation over time — a protocol to monitor closely.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock works but has a higher proportion of non-self listeners (clients, people being supported), which may affect how scoring patterns develop.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight provides good sleep support with most people settling within 5-10 minutes; music opinions lean more neutral than its siblings.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember works for creative flow but shows modest improvement levels with some listeners reporting only slight gains; the rhythm feels balanced.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current is the mid-tier creative flow protocol with notably better outcomes than Level 1, and more people love the music here.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora shows the strongest creative flow results in the entire series despite having fewer responses — a promising protocol worth more exposure.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass is the entry-level focus protocol with moderate outcomes; some listeners find the rhythm slightly too active for deep concentration.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint shows meaningful improvement over Level 1 for focus, with most people settling around 10 minutes and balanced rhythm opinions.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit is the highest-performing focus protocol across all series, with the most 'significant improvement' reports and strong listener satisfaction.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone works well but takes longer to settle (20 minutes is typical); designed for ongoing maintenance rather than quick results.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis shows the widest variance in maintenance outcomes — works great for some but less consistently for others, indicating possible subgroup effects.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit has some listeners who don't settle at all, which is unusual for maintenance; may need adjustment for the non-responding subgroup.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern is a solid mind clarity protocol with two listeners noting things felt worse — unusual but worth monitoring given small sample.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight shows strong 'significant improvement' signals despite moderate average scores, with most listeners finding it very calming.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos is the clear underperformer — the lowest-scoring protocol overall. It may need redesign or better routing to the right listener subgroup.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath has the lowest positive response rate in TRANSFORMING; motivation protocols are harder to deliver consistently.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail shows an interesting split where people love the music but report more moderate improvement; a possible design mismatch.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback is the highest TRANSFORMING mean but with high variance — works very well for some but less so for others, indicating population split.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze is a mood and energy protocol with strong cross-series signals — its AutoGluon score suggests it may help low-affect states better than designed.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise provides solid mood support with strong music appreciation; seems to share design elements with Glacier in the overstimulation series.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft shows the highest 'no effect' rate in TRANSFORMING — may need rhythm adjustment for better entrainment consistency.
